chunks.text: On the last evening of the year, I stopped by a small shrine.
I had visited here at the same time last year.
This is my moment of reflection.
Looking back on the year, I think I ought to have been kinder to others.
I ought not to have said some of the words I said.
The younger me used to be short tempered.
I used to snap at my sister almost every day.
Whenever I recall that, I feel ashamed.
But my sister would always consider me quietly in return.
In the new year, I want to be more modest.
I want to express more gratitude to my family.
I want to adopt a small habit: to rise a little earlier and breathe deeply.
I joined my hands and quietly recalled my late grandfather.
He used to say, " A person does not need to speak many words.
" I want to inherit that quiet advice now.
